exemplar.title: To what extent have the economic rules set in place by the English Premier League effectively impacted market power and competitive balance between 2014 and 2024?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent have the economic rules set in place by the English Premier League effectively impacted market power and competitive balance between 2014 and 2024?

subject.title: Economics
subject.description:
**IB Economics** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **microeconomics, macroeconomics, the global economy, and development economics**, with a strong focus on **analytical and evaluative skills, real-world application of economic theory, and quantitative analysis**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Extended response questions | 1h 15min | 1h 30min | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Data response questions | 1h 45min | 1h 45min | 40% (SL), 30% (HL) |
| **Paper 3** | Mathematical questions | N/A | 1h | 20% (HL only) |
| **Internal Assessment** | Portfolio of 3 commentaries | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: The investigation formulates a clear, time-bounded research question tightly linked to each analytical section
Topic and purpose are communicated accurately and consistently in the introduction, body and table of contents
Methodology outlines a relevant mix of primary and secondary sources with explicit discussion of strengths and limitations
weaknesses: Introduction ambiguously refers to IA vs EE and presents multiple sub-questions, diluting focus
Sampling rationale (club and season selection) is underdeveloped
Data-cleaning procedures and reliability tests for Transfermarkt estimates are omitted
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place this work firmly in the top band (5â6): the topic and focus are accurately communicated, the research question is clear and effectively connected, and the methodology shows informed source selection. Minor ambiguities around report type, sub-questions and data integrity prevent the very highest mark, so a score of 5 is warranted.

strengths: Tables, graphs and quotations are drawn from clearly relevant primary and secondary sources
Coherent application of oligopoly theory, Gini coefficients and externalities to interpret data
Consistent and largely accurate use of specialized economic terminology
weaknesses: Occasional conceptual slips (e.g., mislabelling Gini curve areas)
Reliance on non-authoritative sources for definitions (e.g., Wikipedia)
Conflation of externality impacts in welfare analysis
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Performance sits in the 3â4 band: source material is mostly relevant and well integrated, knowledge of economic disciplines is clear, and terminology is used accurately overall. Minor inaccuracies and source choices preclude top-band status, justifying a mark of 4.

strengths: Majority of research is appropriate and clearly relevant to the research question
Analysis is clear, with well-supported conclusions drawn from Lorenz curves, Gini coefficients and financial trends
Argument is reasoned, with partially critical evaluation of PSR loopholes and reform proposals
weaknesses: Causal links between PSR implementation and competitive balance are asserted more than rigorously proven
Club-specific examples to illustrate regulatory loopholes are limited
Alternative explanations (UEFA rules, management quality) and methodological caveats are acknowledged only briefly
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Across the three strands this work exhibits characteristics of the 7â9 band: research is largely appropriate and relevant; analysis is clear with supported conclusions; and evaluation is reasoned. While deeper causal proof and more critical appraisal would push it higher, a highâband score of 9 accurately reflects the overall strength of critical thinking.

strengths: Report follows IB conventions with logical sectioning, pagination and a comprehensive table of contents
Graphs and tables are placed near relevant discussion, aiding readability
Conclusions synthesize multiple analytical strands coherently
weaknesses: Inconsistent figure numbering and missing axis labels/legends in key charts
Table alignments are uneven in the table of contents
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation meets the 3â4 band overall: structure and layout largely follow conventions and aid navigation. However, recurring formatting lapses (figure labelling, table alignment) mean the work falls short of the very top standard, warranting a score of 3.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research question â âTo what extent have the economic rules set in place by the English Premier League effectively impacted market power and competitive balance between 2014 and 2024?â â is clearly stated, time-bounded and tightly linked to the subsequent discussion of financial data, Lorenz curves and transfer-market behaviour.  References back to the question appear in each analytical section, demonstrating effective connection.  Some breadth (three separate outcome dimensions) slightly dilutes focus, hence 5 not 6.

The student identifies a precise economic focus â the effects of Premier League Profit & Sustainability Rules on market power and competitive balance (2014-2024) â and reiterates this purpose in the introduction, table of contents and throughout the body.  The framing is clearly appropriate for an Economics investigation and well-suited to systematic analysis using data, graphs and theory.  Minor ambiguity remains around whether the study is an IA or EE and around the simultaneous inclusion of multiple sub-questions, but overall communication is accurate and purposeful, justifying placement in the top band (5-6) with a score of 5 rather than 6.

Most tables, graphs and quotations are drawn from sources that are clearly relevant to the question (club accounts, Transfermarkt, Premier League handbook).  They are integrated into the argument, although some citations are second-hand (Wikipedia, Planet Football) and reliability of Transfermarkt estimates is acknowledged but not resolved.  Overall application is mostly appropriate, fitting the 3-4 band, top end.

The report follows IB conventions with abstract elements, numbered sections, introduction-methodology-analysis-conclusion, bibliography and appendices.  Headings and pagination align with a logical flow, and graphs are placed near relevant text.  Minor lapses (e.g., inconsistent figure numbering) exist but do not hinder clarity; therefore the top band (3-4) with full 4 marks is appropriate.

Word count, title and page numbers are provided; tables and graphs are readable; citation style is broadly consistent.  A few formatting issues (broken image captions, occasional misaligned tables) prevent a perfect impression, so 3 within the 3-4 band is justified.

The student offers a reasoned argument that PSR is ineffective, discusses loopholes, stakeholder impacts and suggests reforms.  Evaluation is present but partially critical: alternative explanations (management quality, UEFA rules) are acknowledged only briefly, and methodological caveats are limited.  Structure is coherent but not fully sophisticated, fitting the lower end of 4-6 (score 4).

Analysis goes beyond description: Lorenz curves are constructed, Gini coefficients calculated, financial trends interpreted, and theory (oligopoly, externalities) applied.  Conclusions are generally supported by evidence, though causality between PSR and outcomes is asserted more than rigorously proven.  Depth is therefore clear but not consistently sophisticated â mid-upper 4-6 band (score 5).

The essay demonstrates clear knowledge of oligopoly theory, Gini coefficients, externalities and PSR regulations, using them coherently to interpret data.  Occasional conceptual slips (e.g., confusing Area labels in Gini explanation, mis-reporting coefficient values) prevent a fully âeffectiveâ performance, but understanding is more than anecdotal, warranting 4.

The investigation draws on a substantial body of club financials, transfer tables and trophy data that are largely relevant to the RQ.  While a few weaker sources remain, the majority of research is appropriate and clearly linked, satisfying the 4-6 band; limitations in triangulating data and sample justification suggest 5 rather than 6.

The student outlines a generally relevant mix of primary (club reports) and secondary (Transfermarkt, news articles) sources, explains strengths/limitations, and lists economic tools (Lorenz curve, Gini, supply-demand, break-even).  Evidence of informed selection is present but not fully developed: sampling rationale, reliability tests and data-cleaning procedures are limited, and some important variables (e.g., wage bills, UEFA constraints) are omitted.  This aligns with the 3-4 band; the breadth and partial justification merit the upper value (4).

Economic terminology such as âallocative inefficiency,â âcollusive oligopoly,â âbreak-even,â and âdeadweight lossâ is used frequently and largely accurately.  Minor inaccuracies (e.g., describing area A+B in Gini calculation) occur, but consistency across 4,000 words indicates mostly accurate command â appropriate for the 3-4 band, high side.

annotations.comment: The title is prominently stated at the start, clearly communicating the investigationâs focus on Premier League financial rules and competition.
The introduction ambiguously references whether this is an IA or EE and multiple sub-questions; clarify the report type and consolidate sub-questions to sharpen focus.
Word count is provided and within the 4 000-word limit, satisfying IB formatting requirements.
The table of contents is comprehensive and follows academic conventions, aiding readability and navigation.
The definition of allocative inefficiency is cited from Wikipedia, which is insufficiently authoritative; replace with a peer-reviewed source.
Figure 1 lacks axis labels and units for revenue, losses, and net debt; add clear labels and a legend to enhance interpretability.
The assertion of pre-2014 stability overlooks earlier fluctuations visible in the graph; refine analysis to acknowledge anomalies.
Figure 2 is presented without axis labels or a legend; revise the caption and include methodological notes on data smoothing.
The research question is clearly time-bounded and tightly linked to subsequent analysis, demonstrating focused inquiry into PSR impacts from 2014â2024.
The TOC table spans an extensive area but cell alignment is inconsistent; refine table formatting to improve clarity.
The introduction defines market power clearly and connects it to PSR context, demonstrating relevant economic understanding.
The rationale for selecting Everton as a case study should clarify its representativeness to avoid a potentially anomalous example.
The methodology section outlines primary and secondary sources with benefits and limitations, showing informed source selection.
Sampling rationale for data selection is underdeveloped; explain why specific clubs and seasons were chosen to support validity.
The report omits data-cleaning procedures and reliability tests for Transfermarkt estimates; outline steps taken to ensure data integrity.
Consider discussing UEFA Financial Fair Play in methodology to contextualize overlapping regulatory influences on club finances.
Supply and demand curves are introduced without accompanying diagrams; include a graph or schematic to illustrate theoretical points.
The list of negative externalities conflates welfare impacts; more precise linkage between externality graphs and social welfare theory is needed.
Direct quotations from the Premier League handbook effectively ground the PSR description in primary regulatory text.
When listing PSR loopholes, provide club-specific examples to critically evaluate the practical impact of each exception.
Illustrate numerically how amortisation exclusions influence PSR breaching thresholds by including a worked example in the text or appendix.
Reflection in the acknowledgement section is descriptive; deepen evaluation by discussing why certain methodological adjustments were made in response to challenges.
How were trendlines fitted to the sporadic spending data for Manchester United and City? Clarify smoothing techniques or regression methods.
Applying Lorenz curves to trophy distribution is an appropriate economic tool and connects data to competitive balance effectively.
The formula for the Gini coefficient is presented inline but should be rendered in LaTeX for academic precision.
Clarify the conceptual distinction between area A and area B in the Lorenz curve to avoid misunderstandings in the Gini calculation.
The discussion of oligopoly and collusion graphs is relevant but lacks critical appraisal of their applicability to the Premier League transfer market.
The conclusion synthesizes financial, competitive and market-power dimensions coherently, offering a clear final position on PSR effectiveness.
Proposed future improvements (e.g., price cap) warrant discussion of feasibility and potential unintended consequences to strengthen evaluative insight.
Conclusion sections effectively weave together findings from multiple analyses, demonstrating strong structural coherence.
Citation style varies significantly between bibliography entries and in-text references; adopt a consistent style (e.g., APA) throughout.
Some URLs in the bibliography break across lines, reducing readability; reformat long links to avoid erratic line breaks.
